title: Jain-Style Cabbage and Peas Subzi
description: A simple, light, and low-calorie Jain-friendly dish made without onions or garlic.
explanation: This is a light no-onion/no-garlic sabzi that works with roti, dal, rice, or as a side in a larger thali. Cabbage gives volume, peas add sweetness, and the tempering carries most of the flavor.
Because the ingredient list is short, cooking texture matters. Keep the cabbage slightly tender rather than mushy unless you prefer a softer homestyle sabzi.
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them pop. Add cumin seeds and hing.
Add chopped cabbage and green peas. Saute for 2 minutes.
Add turmeric and salt. Mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender.
stepNotes: Keep the heat moderate so the spices bloom without burning. Curry leaves or grated ginger can be added here if you use them.
A short saute helps the cabbage lose its raw smell before steaming.
Add chilli powder, coriander powder, or black pepper if you want more heat.
Cook less for crunch and longer for a softer sabzi. If the pan dries out, sprinkle a little water instead of adding more oil.
Pair with dal, tofu, chana, or peanut curd if this needs to become a higher-protein meal.

variationSections.title: Vegetable swaps
items: Use carrot, beans, capsicum, cauliflower, or bottle gourd with the same basic tempering.
Replace peas with sprouts, edamame, or boiled chana if you want more protein.
Add grated coconut at the end for a South Indian-style finish.

variationSections.title: No-onion flavor boosters
items: Use hing, ginger, curry leaves, lemon, roasted cumin, or coriander to keep the sabzi lively.
A small spoon of peanut powder or sesame powder can add richness.
Finish with lemon only after turning off the heat so the flavor stays fresh.
